seoTitle: How to Sync Trello With Apple Calendar
metaDescription: Stream your Trello due dates into Apple Calendar using a native iCalendar feed link, synced across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.
intro: Instead of manually copying deadlines between apps, Trello can generate a read-only calendar feed that streams your due dates directly into Apple Calendar. This guide covers activating that feed and subscribing to it on your Apple devices.

steps.heading: Open your board's calendar settings
steps.body: Navigate to the board options menu to find the calendar streaming utility.

steps.heading: Activate the iCal subscription
steps.body: Turn on the calendar feed to generate your secure, personalized iCalendar (iCal) URL.

steps.heading: Copy your feed link
steps.body: Grab the encrypted URL token generated by Trello for your board.

steps.heading: Subscribe in Apple Calendar
steps.body: Open Apple Calendar on macOS or iOS and add the subscription using the URL you copied.

steps.heading: Adjust your refresh interval
steps.body: Set how often the feed refreshes to keep your devices up to date without affecting performance.

steps.heading: Keep your link private
steps.body: Treat your calendar feed URL as sensitive, since anyone with the link can view your project's due dates.

faq.question: Is this sync two-way?
faq.answer: No, this is a one-way, read-only feed that streams Trello due dates into Apple Calendar, not the other way around.

faq.question: Does this work across all my Apple devices?
faq.answer: Yes, once you subscribe using the iCal link, the feed syncs across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

faq.question: How often does the calendar update?
faq.answer: You can adjust the sync refresh interval in Apple Calendar to control how often it checks for updates.

faq.question: Should I keep my calendar feed link private?
faq.answer: Yes, anyone with your unique feed link can view your project's due dates, so it's worth keeping it secure.
